selection_policy: Only source-backed Kimi K3 workflows, integrations, observations, and limits from the supplied folder are published. Missing prompts and results are never reconstructed.

items.title: Build a Voxel Pod Racer From One Prompt
items.takeaway: Use a short scene concept to prototype a playable voxel pod-racing experience, then plan a second pass for racers, a finish line, and detail review.
items.body_notes: The creator reports that Kimi K3 produced version one from a single public prompt and says version two would add more racers, a finish line, and detail review.
items.decision_reason: Concrete generated artifact, exact public prompt, visible demo media, and an explicit next iteration boundary.
items.prompt_boundary: The exact reusable prompt is present in the supplied source.

items.title: Compare Frogger Builds With the Same Prompt
items.takeaway: Hold the prompt constant when comparing two model outputs so the resulting gameplay difference is easier to inspect.
items.body_notes: The creator shares a Kimi K3 Frogger version as a same-prompt comparison between two models. The supplied source does not publish the prompt text or evaluation rubric.
items.decision_reason: A controlled same-prompt comparison with visible output and a clear missing-rubric caveat.
items.prompt_boundary: The source says the prompt was the same but does not publish it; omit the prompt block.

items.title: Generate a Frogger Game and Its Recording
items.takeaway: Test a one-shot game build together with a separate one-shot workflow for recording the resulting gameplay.
items.body_notes: The creator reports that Kimi K3 produced the Frogger game in one shot and also produced the gameplay recording workflow in one shot. The exact prompts are not included.
items.decision_reason: Two concrete outputs are stated and accompanied by gameplay media, with the absent prompt boundary preserved.
items.prompt_boundary: No exact reusable prompt text is present in the supplied source; omit the prompt block.

items.title: Prototype an Aircraft Carrier in Three.js
items.takeaway: Use a concrete launch-and-recovery scene to probe Kimi K3's ability to produce an interactive Three.js 3D prototype.
items.body_notes: The creator demonstrates a Three.js scene of fighter-jet launch and recovery on a Nimitz-class aircraft carrier and describes Kimi K3 as strong for 3D generation.
items.decision_reason: Named 3D environment, concrete interaction scenario, and visible demo media.
items.prompt_boundary: The source includes a scene description but does not explicitly label it as the complete prompt; omit the prompt block.

items.title: Create an Interactive Motion Graphic Frontend
items.takeaway: Use a one-shot code-generation test to build motion graphics that remain interactive when the animation is paused.
items.body_notes: The creator reports a 42-minute, one-shot build using simple code without a harness, MCP, or skills, and demonstrates a motion graphic whose design remains interactive when paused.
items.decision_reason: Specific duration, tool boundary, interaction behavior, and visible output.
items.prompt_boundary: No exact reusable prompt text is present in the supplied source; omit the prompt block.

items.title: Produce a Synchronized Motion Graphic Ad
items.takeaway: Evaluate a one-shot motion-graphics workflow by checking whether music, effects, and motion stay synchronized in the final ad.
items.body_notes: The creator reports that a pure-prompt, one-shot Kimi K3 run produced a Spotify-themed motion graphic ad with synchronized music, effects, and motion. The exact prompt is not included.
items.decision_reason: Concrete advertising output, explicit one-shot boundary, and visible synchronized media.
items.prompt_boundary: The source says pure prompt only but does not publish the prompt; omit the prompt block.

items.title: Access Kimi K3 Through Kimi CLI
items.takeaway: Use the official Kimi CLI as one available interface for Kimi K3 after checking the current CLI documentation and model selector.
items.body_notes: The creator announces Kimi K3 availability in Kimi CLI and shares a screenshot. This repository does not infer installation or authentication steps from the announcement.
items.decision_reason: Named product integration with visual evidence and a clearly limited setup claim.
items.prompt_boundary: No prompt applies to this integration announcement.

items.title: Observe OpenRouter Throughput via Moonshot API
items.takeaway: Record provider, route, and observed tokens per second when comparing Kimi K3 serving performance.
items.body_notes: One user reports Kimi K3 running on OpenRouter at 28 tokens per second through the Moonshot API. Treat this as a single observation, not a benchmark or service guarantee.
items.decision_reason: Named serving route and numeric observation with an explicit single-sample caveat.
items.prompt_boundary: No prompt applies to this serving observation.

items.title: Test Vision Quality on Your Own Workload
items.takeaway: Evaluate Kimi K3 vision on representative tasks from your own workload instead of generalizing from one user's experience.
items.body_notes: Armin Ronacher reports that Kimi K3's vision quality felt strong for his workloads and that he would not notice the absence of a model he considers state of the art. This is a personal workload observation, not a benchmark.
items.decision_reason: Experienced practitioner observation with an explicit personal-workload scope.
items.prompt_boundary: No exact task or prompt is published; omit the prompt block.

items.title: Watch for Looping at Maximum Effort
items.takeaway: Set stopping criteria and review checkpoints when a maximum-effort agent run repeatedly revisits and tweaks completed work.
items.body_notes: Ethan Mollick reports that a Kimi K3 model-and-harness setup repeatedly looped back over tasks to tweak and change them at maximum effort. The source does not isolate whether the behavior came from the model, harness, or their interaction.
items.decision_reason: Concrete operational limitation with the model-versus-harness attribution uncertainty preserved.
items.prompt_boundary: No exact task or prompt is published; omit the prompt block.
